name: Safety Transportation
service_types: unclear - likely livery/van transport, possibly school
recent_news: No public news found. Yelp listing exists for Safety Transportation in Lawrence, MA. Multiple Nunez-affiliated transport entities operate in Lawrence/Boston area (Nunez Livery, Nunez Transportation 978, Nunez Elite Transportation) - mostly taxi/livery/limo rather than school bus contracting.
fit_notes: Very small (~12 employees, ~$516K rev), no confirmed school-bus contractor profile. Lawrence MA market is dominated by NRT Bus and Beacon Mobility (which holds LPS electric bus contract). Likely sub-scale livery/van operator - low priority tuck-in unless verified as SPED/McKinney-Vento niche.

name: Merry-Go-Round Transportation
recent_news: NJ-incorporated 1995, small family-owned operator at 124 Little Falls Rd, Fairfield NJ. Listed as school bus transportation plus charter/motorcoach. No M&A or news activity found.
fit_notes: 30+ year-old NJ family operator with female President - potential succession candidate. Very small scale (~10 employees, ~$516K rev). Essex County NJ overlaps Summit's Northeast footprint. Likely too small for primary tuck-in, but bolt-on to a larger NJ acquisition could absorb routes. Charter/rental mix dilutes pure-school-bus appeal.

name: Kids To & From School
recent_news: No web presence located. Phone (609) 835-9063 is in the 609 area code (central/south NJ - Mercer/Burlington/Ocean). No company website, listings, or news found. Name suggests child shuttle / private after-school transport rather than yellow-bus district contractor.
fit_notes: Essentially no digital footprint. Very small (~11 employees, ~$514K rev). Without confirmation of district contracts or fleet, not actionable as a tuck-in. Could be a mom-and-pop child-shuttle service rather than a school-bus contractor. Skip unless phone outreach confirms district contracts.

name: White Plains Bus Co & Suburban
owner_or_parent: Summit School Services (formerly National Express LLC; I Squared Capital portfolio)
recent_news: White Plains Bus Co. is a subsidiary of National Express LLC / Summit School Services - the Summit platform itself. The nellc.com/whiteplains page now 301-redirects to summitllc.com/whiteplains, confirming the rebrand. Old Greenwich CT address at 115 Laddins Rock Rd is a White Plains Bus depot/staging facility serving Greenwich/Fairfield County. Founded 1922.
fit_notes: NOT A TARGET - this is the Summit/National Express platform itself. White Plains Bus Co. and Suburban operations are already part of the Summit family. Exclude from tuck-in pipeline.

name: JD Padilla School Bus LLC
service_types: school bus (route contractor to Quemado Independent Schools, likely)
districts_or_customers: Quemado Independent Schools (likely - sole district in Catron County NM)
recent_news: No public news, website, or M&A activity found. Address '13 mi W 2 mi N of Quemado' indicates a single-family rural NM route contractor operating in Catron County. Quemado ISD documents confirm students are bused from outlying communities, consistent with small independent route contractor model used in rural NM.
fit_notes: Extremely rural single-route family contractor in Catron County NM (population ~3,500 county-wide). NM is outside Summit's core footprint and no expansion-state flag. Sub-scale (~12 employees, ~$504K rev) with single-district dependency. Not attractive as standalone tuck-in; geographic isolation makes route consolidation impractical.
